issue_title: Retry loops with feedback — pass failure context to retry attempts
issue_description: Add {failure_context} template variable that contains the previous attempt's error details when a phase is being retried. This builds on the existing retry logic from #209 (retries, retry_delay_seconds fields). The key addition is that retry attempts receive the error message from the previous attempt so the LLM can learn from the failure and try a different approach.
acceptance_criteria: New template variable {failure_context} available in prompt_template interpolation
On first attempt, {failure_context} resolves to empty string
On retry attempts, {failure_context} contains: attempt number, error message from previous attempt, any partial output from previous attempt
Retry history tracked in phase result metadata: list of {attempt, error, partial_output, tokens_used}
The failure_context format is human-readable markdown suitable for LLM consumption
Backward compatible: templates without {failure_context} work unchanged
Tests cover: first attempt (empty context), retry with error feedback, retry with partial output, multiple retries accumulating history
codebase_context: The sequencer (sequencer.py) already has retry logic in _execute_and_wait() from PR #215 (#209). It tracks attempt number and last_error. The _build_phase_input() method handles template variable interpolation using SafeDict. We need to: (1) capture error details between attempts, (2) format them as {failure_context}, (3) make them available to _build_phase_input(). The retry loop is at approximately line 409 of sequencer.py. PhaseDefinition already has retries and retry_delay_seconds fields.
files_to_modify: src/orchestration_engine/sequencer.py — add failure_context to retry loop and _build_phase_input
constraints: Build on existing retry logic from #209 — do NOT rewrite the retry loop
failure_context must be safe for prompt injection (no raw error tracebacks, sanitize)
Keep the retry loop in _execute_and_wait() — add failure tracking around it
The {failure_context} variable follows the same SafeDict pattern as other template vars
Retry history should be stored in the phase result dict under 'retry_history' key
